LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF DAVID ACHTERKIRCHEN'S MOTION FOR AN AWARD OF ATTORNEYS FEES ON APPEAL PURSUANT TO CALIFORNIA CIVIL CODE SECTION 1717 IS GRANTED IN PART, AND DENIED IN PART. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES ON APPEAL, PURSUANT TO CALIFORNIA CIVIL CODE SECTION 1717, IS GRANTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $16,800. ALTHOUGH PLAINTIFFS DID NOT PROVIDE COPIES OF INVOICES ASSOCIATED WITH THE MATTER, MR. HERMAN'S DECLARATION OUTLINED THE SPECIFIC TASKS PERFORMED IN THE MATTER, THE ATTORNEY WHO PERFORMED THE TASKS, THE TOTAL NUMBER OF HOURS SPENT ON THE TASKS, AND THE ATTORNEY'S HOURLY BILLING RATE. (HERMAN DECL. PARAGRAPHS 5-6.) PLAINTIFFS ALLEGE THAT ALL OF THE TASKS PERFORMED WERE NECESSARY FOR THEM TO SUCCEED IN THE MATTER. PLAINTIFFS HAVE PROVIDED SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT THEIR CLAIM FOR ATTORNEY FEES IN THE APPEAL MATTER AND DEFENDANT HAS NOT MEET HIS BURDEN TO SHOW THAT THE AMOUNT IS UNREASONABLE. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR AN AWARD OF $4,800 IN ATTORNEY FEES, FOR FILING AND PROSECUTING THIS MOTION AND APPEARING IN ORAL ARGUMENT, IS DENIED. THE COURT APPROVES FEES OF $1,500 AS A REASONABLE AMOUNT FOR PRESENTATION OF ARGUMENT IN MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER : MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489 =302/AJR

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant Montiel's 2 issues are addressed on the motion to tax costs. Defendant cites CCP 15.10 re: attorney's fees. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling with modification as follows:

PLAINTIFFS DAVID ACHTERKIRCHEN, JANE ACHTERKIRCHEN, GENNA TRICARICO, CHRISTOPHER PADULA, BLAKE MUELLER, MATTEO FABIANO'S Motion For Order Striking Or Taxing Costs.

Plaintiffs' motion for an order striking or taxing costs is denied in substantial part. None of the costs claimed by defendant Jesus Montiel on his memorandum of costs on appeal are taxed or stricken. All $1870.50 of those costs are allowed, even though some of those costs were not actually paid by Mr. Montiel as a result of receiving a fee waiver. The $1870.50 is a reduction off the amount of the judgment in favor of plaintiffs. Mr. Montiel has presented sufficient evidence to show that his costs memorandum was timely filed. This motion was also timely filed. A party who obtains a fee waiver is nonetheless entitled to recovery of costs covered by the fee waiver, but is obligated to pay those costs to the court that issued the waiver.

The tentative ruling is adopted and modified to include the language as stated on the record in respect to set-off and interest. RULING - THE MATTER WAS CALLED TWICE ON THE RECORD AND WAS ARGUED REGARDING WHETHER RETIRED JUDGE KRAMER SHOULD HEAR THIS MATTER OR NOT. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF DAVID ACHTERKIRCHEN's Motion For An Award Of Attorneys Fees On Appeal Pursuant To California Civil Code Section 1717 - Motion is granted in part, and denied in part. Plaintiffs' motion for attorney fees on appeal, pursuant to California Civil Code section 1717, is granted in the amount of $16,800. Although Plaintiffs did not provide copies of invoices associated with the matter, Mr. Herman's declaration outlined the specific tasks performed in the matter, the attorney who performed the tasks, the total number of hours spent on the tasks, and the attorney's hourly billing rate. (Herman Decl. paragraphs 5-6.) Plaintiffs allege that all of the tasks performed were necessary for them to succeed in the matter. Plaintiffs have provided sufficient evidence to support their claim for attorney fees in the appeal matter and Defendant has not meet his burden to show that the amount is unreasonable. Plaintiffs' motion for an award of $4,800 in attorney fees, for filing and prosecuting this motion and appearing in oral argument, is denied. The Court approves fees of $1,500 as a reasonable amount for presentation of argument in motion for attorney fees. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: A.
